assertion_results.evidence: Uses SQL/pipe syntax: events | filter ... | join(...)  — completely wrong syntax

assertion_results.text: Query uses two distinct event variables like $e1/$e2
assertion_results.evidence: No $e1/$e2 event variables — uses SQL join syntax

assertion_results.text: Query joins on a shared placeholder variable
assertion_results.evidence: Uses SQL ON login_user = deletion_user — not YARA-L placeholder syntax

assertion_results.text: First event filters for USER_LOGIN with ALLOW action
assertion_results.evidence: Filters for eventStatus = SUCCESS instead of security_result.action = ALLOW — wrong field
FILE_DELETION is referenced in the SQL join target

assertion_results.text: Query uses match: with over 30m window
Query uses condition: with both event variables
assertion_results.evidence: No condition: section — uses SQL WHERE clause

assertion_results.text: Query does NOT write a YARA-L rule { } detection rule block
assertion_results.evidence: No rule { } block written — but the query syntax used is wrong (SQL-like)
